sources.notes: Choose an integer discount divisor x and maximize new prices minus the cost of printed replacement tags.
Official reference code enumerates x and uses frequency prefix sums to count prices with each new ceil(c/x) value.

annotation.summary: Choose a divisor x>1 for all prices, then maximize total discounted prices minus the number of newly printed price tags times y.
annotation.constraints: c_i<=2e5 and total n<=2e5, so enumeration over the value bound and harmonic bucket ranges is feasible.
annotation.core_idea: For a fixed x, all original prices c in ((p-1)x, px] become new price p. Frequency prefix sums give the count in each bucket, and the number of tags that must be printed for price p is max(0, new_count[p]-old_count[p]). Enumerating all x and all nonempty ceil-division buckets gives the best income.
annotation.complexity: O(C log C) per test case where C=max(c_i), plus O(n+C) preprocessing.

solution_variants.name: enumerate-divisor-buckets
solution_variants.summary: Build value frequencies and prefix sums, enumerate x, and sum every ceil(c/x) bucket's income and printing cost.

tags.evidence: The solution directly enumerates every possible divisor x because prices are bounded by 2e5.
Prefix sums over price frequencies are required to count each ceil-division bucket quickly.
For each x, values are grouped by the quotient ceil(c/x), and only these quotient buckets are evaluated.
